Pythia/API.lean.rationale: Public re-export surface only. No theorem declarations; pure namespace re-export via transitive imports. Structural glue.

Pythia/Actuarial.lean.rationale: Umbrella import-only module; aggregates Actuarial sub-modules. No theorems declared here.

Pythia/Actuarial/LogNormal.lean.regime: log-normal with log-mean mu and log-std sigma > 0; tail bound via Chebyshev
Pythia/Actuarial/LogNormal.lean.rationale: User-facing actuarial loss distribution. tail_chebyshev is closed (uses Chebyshev); mean/variance/median are scaffold-sorrys. Working actuaries test log-normal tails empirically. Differential: Chebyshev bound is computable from sigma, mu, t.

Pythia/Actuarial/Pareto.lean.regime: Pareto Type-I with scale x_m > 0 and shape alpha > 0; closed tail formula
Pythia/Actuarial/Pareto.lean.rationale: Standard insurance heavy-tail model. tail formula is closed; mean/variance/median are scaffold-sorrys. Empirical: verify survival function (x_m/t)^alpha numerically across alpha, t.

Pythia/Actuarial/Weibull.lean.regime: Weibull with scale lambda > 0 and shape k > 0; closed tail formula
Pythia/Actuarial/Weibull.lean.rationale: Standard reliability / actuarial lifetime model. tail formula exp(-(t/lambda)^k) is closed; mean/variance/median scaffold-sorrys. Empirical: test tail values across lambda, k, t.

Pythia/AsymptoticSharpness.lean.regime: asymptotic (T -> infinity) regime for aCS family; CLT-based
Pythia/AsymptoticSharpness.lean.rationale: Spine infrastructure for the aCS sharp-constant claim. The headline theorem carries an honest sorry; helper lemmas closed. Part of the anytime-valid CS machinery tested in the confidence_sequence spine sim.

Pythia/Asymptotics/DeltaMethod.lean.regime: scalar delta method; r_n(T_n - theta) ->_d Z, g differentiable at theta
Pythia/Asymptotics/DeltaMethod.lean.rationale: Core asymptotic statistics result used for confidence intervals on nonlinear functions of estimators. Every working statistician uses this. Empirical: sample normal estimates, apply g, check asymptotic distribution matches g'(theta)*Z.

Pythia/Asymptotics/DeltaMethodMulti.lean.regime: multivariate delta method; Frechet differentiable g at theta
Pythia/Asymptotics/DeltaMethodMulti.lean.rationale: Multivariate extension of delta method. Used for GMM, odds ratios, implied vol. Empirical: simulate multivariate normal T_n, apply nonlinear g, check J*Z distribution.

Pythia/AxiomAudit.lean.rationale: Axiom-audit harness using #print axioms. No new theorems; pure CI-facing diagnostic infrastructure.

Pythia/BDG.lean.regime: p=2; discrete-time martingale with M_0=0; finite second moment
Pythia/BDG.lean.rationale: User-facing concentration result for L^2 martingale norm. Empirical: sample bounded-increment martingale paths, check BDG bound holds at multiple alpha levels. Differential test feasible because BDG constant 4 is computable in Lean.

Pythia/Basic.lean.rationale: Foundational types only: BitPrecision structure, Time abbrev, slack noncomputable def. No theorem declarations.

Pythia/Bench/MiniPythia.lean.rationale: Benchmark tactic-closure suite. Each entry tests that a tactic closes a goal; the theorems are tactic-closure demonstrations, not user-facing applied math results. Infrastructure.

Pythia/BenchDefs.lean.rationale: Definitions of sharp constants (c_HR_sharp, c_betting_sharp), CSFamily structure, boundary function. Primarily def/structure declarations; small helper lemmas (c_sharp_ranking, ranking_four_way) are internal infrastructure for BenchTargets.

Pythia/BenchTargets.lean.rationale: Test-fixture stubs for the 48-target drafter slate. Each bench_ theorem is a restatement of an existing library theorem under a bench_ prefix. Not new mathematical content; benchmark scaffolding.

Pythia/Bennett.lean.regime: iid bounded RVs with |X_i| <= b, E[X]=0, E[X^2] <= sigma^2
Pythia/Bennett.lean.rationale: Tail bound for iid bounded RVs using the Bennett phi function. Shares concentration_iid_bounded sim with sub-gaussian, sub-gamma, Hoeffding, Bernstein. Tighter than Bernstein at large deviations.

Pythia/Bernstein.lean.regime: iid bounded RVs or bounded-increment martingales; variance-aware regime
Pythia/Bernstein.lean.rationale: Bernstein's inequality + martingale extension. bernstein_of_subGamma is fully closed; iid/martingale variants are scaffold-sorrys. Part of concentration_iid_bounded spine sim with Bennett, Hoeffding.

Pythia/BettingCS.lean.regime: betting CS; sub-Gaussian; log-wealth threshold; infinite horizon
Pythia/BettingCS.lean.rationale: User-facing betting confidence sequence construction. Admissibility theorem gives anytime-valid coverage guarantee. Empirical: simulate betting wealth process, verify stopping probability <= alpha.

Pythia/BettingStrategy.lean.rationale: BettingStrategy structure + wealthProcess definition. Infrastructure for BettingCS; no user-facing applied-math theorems.

Pythia/Bio.lean.rationale: Umbrella import-only module aggregating Bio sub-modules. No theorems.

Pythia/Bio/MassAction.lean.regime: chemical reaction network ODEs under mass-action kinetics; scaffold-sorry
Pythia/Bio/MassAction.lean.rationale: Foundational CRN ODE results. All scaffold-sorrys but the theorem shapes are substantive applied math (comp-bio, chemical engineering). Empirical: simulate mass-action ODE, verify nonnegativity and conservation. BORDERLINE: all content is scaffold-sorry.

Pythia/Bio/MichaelisMentenSaturation.lea.rationale: Closed fully proven bound. User-facing biochemistry result: reaction velocity bounded by Vmax. Already in theorem_manifest. Empirical: sweep Vmax, Km, S; verify saturation bound.

Pythia/Bio/Phylogenetics.lean.rationale: Scaffold stubs only: felsenstein_correct_spec is trivial (True := by trivial). JukesCantor_pi_sum is a tiny algebraic lemma. No substantive closed theorems. BORDERLINE.

Pythia/Bio/Population.lean.regime: population genetics and predator-prey dynamics; closed algebraic proofs
Pythia/Bio/Population.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed results covering Hardy-Weinberg, Lotka-Volterra equilibria, and SIR conservation. Working scientists in biology test these empirically.

Pythia/Chemistry/Arrhenius.lean.regime: Arrhenius equation; A > 0, T > 0, R > 0, any Ea
Pythia/Chemistry/Arrhenius.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: rate constant positivity. Empirical: sweep A, Ea, R, T; verify positivity.

Pythia/Chemistry/HendersonHasselbalch.le.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed monotonicity result for Henderson-Hasselbalch. Empirical: sweep ratio, verify pH monotonicity.

Pythia/Chemistry/MassActionConservation..regime: single reversible reaction A <-> B; conservation of moles
Pythia/Chemistry/MassActionConservation..rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed trivial algebraic identity. Differential test not interesting (it's rfl-level). Still Tier-A as the empirical harness already exists.

Pythia/Control/Lyapunov.lean.regime: scalar linear system dx/dt = -alpha*x with alpha > 0
Pythia/Control/Lyapunov.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed results. Working control engineers verify Lyapunov stability empirically.

Pythia/Control/LyapunovDiscrete.lean.regime: discrete-time system x_{n+1}=f(x_n); Lyapunov V positive-definite on closed ball
Pythia/Control/LyapunovDiscrete.lean.rationale: Full discrete-time Lyapunov asymptotic stability proof. User-facing control result for working engineers and control theorists.

Pythia/Control/LyapunovODE.lean.regime: continuous-time ODE x'=f(x); Lyapunov V, orbital derivative < 0
Pythia/Control/LyapunovODE.lean.rationale: Full continuous-time Lyapunov ODE asymptotic stability. In AxiomAudit. User-facing result for control engineers and ODE practitioners.

Pythia/DeploymentDesign.lean.regime: deployment spec with alpha, delta, sigma; bit-width b and scale s
Pythia/DeploymentDesign.lean.rationale: Inverse quantization-slack bound: given coverage tolerance delta and sigma, compute minimal scale s. Spine infrastructure for the anytime-valid CS deployment framework.

Pythia/EDetector.lean.regime: sequential change detection; e-detector with threshold alpha in (0,1)
Pythia/EDetector.lean.rationale: E-detector framework for anytime-valid change detection. Spine-B: shares the anytime-valid confidence_sequence sim framework. Many theorems are scaffold-sorrys.

Pythia/Economics/CAPM.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result. Empirical: sweep cov, varM; verify beta nonnegativity.

Pythia/Economics/CRRA.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: marginal utility positivity for all risk-aversion parameters.

Pythia/Economics/CobbDouglas.lean.regime: Cobb-Douglas; K, L, lam > 0, alpha in (0,1)
Pythia/Economics/CobbDouglas.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: constant returns to scale. Core macroeconomics result.

Pythia/Economics/RiskNeutralCall.lean.regime: European call; S, K any real, T >= 0, r any real
Pythia/Economics/RiskNeutralCall.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: option price non-negativity.

Pythia/Economics/Walras.lean.regime: n-good economy; market clearing z_i = 0 for all i
Pythia/Economics/Walras.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result (trivial algebra once clearing assumed). The empirical content is the clearing-implies-zero-sum property; differential not interesting.

Pythia/ElegantUnification.lean.rationale: Three information-theoretic identities showing eta rates equal sqrt of Shannon entropy. Closed arithmetic identities (rfl-level), not user-facing applied-math results. Internal structural connections.

Pythia/Engineering/PowerDissipation.lean.regime: resistive power; R >= 0, I any real
Pythia/Engineering/PowerDissipation.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: P = I^2 * R >= 0.

Pythia/Engineering/RCTimeConstant.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: tau = R*C > 0.

Pythia/Engineering/SignalEnergy.lean.regime: discrete-time signal x : Fin n -> R; any signal
Pythia/Engineering/SignalEnergy.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: sum of squares >= 0. Differential not interesting.

Pythia/EquivalenceBreak.lean.rationale: The main theorem equivalence_break_at_finite_precision is documented as FALSE (per the file's own STATUS section with a concrete counterexample). Only definitions and structural stubs. Not classifiable as Tier-A/B until the theorem is corrected. BORDERLINE - flagged for research review.

Pythia/GameTheory/MinimaxTwoStrategyBoun.regime: 2x2 payoff matrix; pure strategies; any real entries
Pythia/GameTheory/MinimaxTwoStrategyBoun.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: max_i min_j A[i][j] <= min_j max_i A[i][j].

Pythia/GaussianRandomWalk.lean.regime: iid Gaussian increments; sub-Gaussian MG on canonical product space
Pythia/GaussianRandomWalk.lean.rationale: Scaffold infrastructure for the sharpness/adversary constructions. gaussianProductMeasure is sorry'd. Spine-B: supports martingale_concentration sim framework.

Pythia/GaussianSmallBall.lean.regime: Gaussian measure; sigma > 0, c in R, epsilon > 0
Pythia/GaussianSmallBall.lean.rationale: Infrastructure lemma: lower bound on Gaussian measure of small intervals. Used in sharpness arguments. Not a direct user-facing result but has clear empirical content (anti-concentration). Shared with confidence_sequence spine.

Pythia/HowardRamdasCS.lean.regime: Howard-Ramdas self-normalized CS; sub-Gaussian; infinite horizon
Pythia/HowardRamdasCS.lean.rationale: User-facing anytime-valid CS construction. Admissibility theorem is the main coverage guarantee. Empirical: simulate sub-Gaussian walks, verify coverage at multiple alpha levels.

Pythia/HypothesisTest/MultipleTesting.le.regime: m simultaneous tests; FWER/FDR at level alpha
Pythia/HypothesisTest/MultipleTesting.le.rationale: Standard multiple-testing corrections used by working scientists in every quantitative field. bonferroni_fwer has a sorry (easy close noted); holm+BH are scaffolds. BORDERLINE: bonferroni is easy to close, others are harder. Flag for research.

Pythia/HypothesisTest/Wald.lean.rationale: wald_alpha_bound_spec and wald_two_sided_alpha_spec are both trivial (True := by trivial). WaldStatistic is a def. No substantive theorems. Pure scaffold.

Pythia/InfoTheory/BinaryEntropy.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: H(p) >= 0. Fundamental information theory.

Pythia/InfoTheory/BretagnolleHuberBinary.regime: binary alphabet; p, q in (0,1); TV distance vs KL divergence
Pythia/InfoTheory/BretagnolleHuberBinary.rationale: Binary-alphabet Bretagnolle-Huber inequality: TV(P,Q) <= sqrt(1 - exp(-KL)). Used in minimax lower bound arguments. Applied information theory / statistics.

Pythia/InfoTheory/DataProcessing.lean.regime: Markov kernel on measurable spaces; nu << mu
Pythia/InfoTheory/DataProcessing.lean.rationale: Data Processing Inequality: processing data cannot increase KL divergence. Fundamental result in information theory / statistics / ML. In AxiomAudit.

Pythia/InfoTheory/HammingDistanceTriangl.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed by decide. Differential not interesting (decidable enumeration).

Pythia/InformationTheoretic.lean.rationale: Information-theoretic formulation of quantization slack. Spine-B: infrastructure for the anytime-valid CS quantization framework. Links Shannon capacity to eta rates.

Pythia/InputQuantization.lean.regime: input-quantized martingale; scale s; SubGaussianMG extended
Pythia/InputQuantization.lean.rationale: QuantizedMartingale struct + input-quantization transport bound. Spine-B: infrastructure for sensor-quantization variant of the deployment-slack framework.

Pythia/MGFBoundedSubGamma.lean.regime: bounded centered RV; |X| <= b, E[X]=0, E[X^2] <= sigma^2, b*lam < 3
Pythia/MGFBoundedSubGamma.lean.rationale: MGF bound that is the missing piece for Bennett/Bernstein closures. Spine-B infrastructure for concentration_iid_bounded; not directly user-facing.

Pythia/MatchingConstants.lean.regime: sharp matching-lower-bound constants for CS families
Pythia/MatchingConstants.lean.rationale: Spine infrastructure: explicit sharp constants for matching lower bounds. Pure arithmetic identities about eta rates; not directly user-facing but part of the CS family spine.

Pythia/MathlibTags.lean.regime: two non-negative reals (AM-GM); general measure space (Markov/Cauchy-Schwarz)
Pythia/MathlibTags.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Retags Mathlib lemmas with @[stat_lemma]. AM-GM and Cauchy-Schwarz are classic applied-math results. Markov inequality is fundamental.

Pythia/MatrixBernstein.lean.regime: independent zero-mean self-adjoint matrices; d-dimensional; ||X_k||_op <= R; sigma^2 variance proxy
Pythia/MatrixBernstein.lean.rationale: Tropp's matrix Bernstein inequality. All sorry'd. Every practitioner in randomized algorithms/ML uses this. BORDERLINE: entirely scaffold-sorry with extensive Mathlib gap inventory. Flag for research.

Pythia/MatrixBernsteinFull.lean.regime: same as MatrixBernstein; Tropp 5-step proof architecture
Pythia/MatrixBernsteinFull.lean.rationale: Full proof module for Tropp's matrix Bernstein. scaffold-sorry on the Laplace step (needs Lieb). Tier-A because the outer optimization step is proved. Shares sim with MatrixBernstein.

Pythia/MatrixLieb.lean.rationale: Spine infrastructure for MatrixBernstein. Several lemmas proved (hermFuncCalc_id, matExp_posDef); core Lieb concavity theorem is sorry'd. Tier-B: building block, not direct user result.

Pythia/MeasureTheory/ConditionalJensen.l.regime: convex phi on R; sub-sigma-algebra m; integrable X
Pythia/MeasureTheory/ConditionalJensen.l.rationale: Conditional Jensen's inequality. In AxiomAudit. Load-bearing for martingale and concentration results. User-facing: working probabilists test Jensen numerically. Shared martingale_concentration spine.

Pythia/MeasureTheory/OptionalStoppingUnb.regime: L1-martingale; a.s.-finite stopping time tau; UI stopped process
Pythia/MeasureTheory/OptionalStoppingUnb.rationale: Fully closed (zero sorries per file header). Unbounded optional stopping theorem - spine infrastructure for Wald identity and SPRT. Tier-B: building block for sequential statistics.

Pythia/MeasureTheory/PathMeasureRN.lean.regime: countable product measures; sigma-finite mu_n, nu_n
Pythia/MeasureTheory/PathMeasureRN.lean.rationale: Path-measure Radon-Nikodym chain rule + Kakutani dichotomy. Several sorry'd gaps; measurable_partialRNDeriv and measurable_rnDeriv_comp are proved. Spine-B: deep infrastructure for sequential statistics.

Pythia/Mechanical/HookeSpring.lean.regime: Hookean spring; k >= 0, x any displacement
Pythia/Mechanical/HookeSpring.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: spring PE >= 0.

Pythia/NewTargetsStubs.lean.rationale: Target stubs for 60-target drafter expansion. T2/T3 theorem stubs that restate existing library results or are arithmetic consequences. Some are proved locally (etaHR_monotone calls etaHR_mono). Infrastructure/scaffolding.

Pythia/Numerical.lean.rationale: Umbrella import-only module aggregating Numerical sub-modules. No theorems.

Pythia/Numerical/KKT.lean.regime: nonlinear program with inequality + equality constraints; Slater CQ or convexity
Pythia/Numerical/KKT.lean.rationale: KKT conditions scaffold. kkt_necessary and kkt_sufficient_convex are scaffold-sorrys. Core applied optimization result used by every engineer. BORDERLINE: structure-only scaffold.

Pythia/Numerical/Kahan.lean.regime: floating-point summation; machine epsilon eps; n-element list
Pythia/Numerical/Kahan.lean.rationale: Kahan compensated summation correctness. scaffold-sorry. Numerical analysts test this empirically on different input distributions. BORDERLINE: Float-vs-Real bridging not in Mathlib.

Pythia/Numerical/Lyapunov.lean.regime: scalar autonomous ODE y'=f(y); Lyapunov V positive-definite
Pythia/Numerical/Lyapunov.lean.rationale: Numerical/Lyapunov is a duplicate scaffold of Control/LyapunovODE but for the scalar case (Numerical.PicardLindelof context). All scaffold-sorrys. Tier-B: spine for the Numerical modules. BORDERLINE vs Control/LyapunovODE.

Pythia/Numerical/NewtonQuadraticIterPos..regime: Newton iteration for x^2 - c; x > 0, c > 0
Pythia/Numerical/NewtonQuadraticIterPos..rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: Newton iterate stays positive.

Pythia/Numerical/PicardLindelof.lean.regime: IVP y'=f(t,y), y(t0)=y0; f Lipschitz in y with constant K
Pythia/Numerical/PicardLindelof.lean.rationale: Picard-Lindelof scaffold. All scaffold-sorrys. Core applied ODE result used by scientists. BORDERLINE: purely scaffold.

Pythia/OR/LittlesLaw.lean.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: L = lam*W >= 0.

Pythia/OptimalTransport/WassersteinDista.regime: discrete L1 Wasserstein; p, q : Fin n -> R
Pythia/OptimalTransport/WassersteinDista.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: sum |p_i - q_i| >= 0.

Pythia/PACBayesCS.lean.regime: PAC-Bayes CS; prior P over strategies; posterior Q; KL(Q||P) penalty
Pythia/PACBayesCS.lean.rationale: PAC-Bayes CS: tighter CS by averaging over strategies with KL penalty. Scaffold-sorrys. User-facing applied Bayesian statistics. BORDERLINE: all sorry'd.

Pythia/PhiTransform.lean.regime: phi-transform from HR to betting form; sigma, alpha, T parameters
Pythia/PhiTransform.lean.rationale: Phi-transform infrastructure: exponential betting-transform from HR to wealth process. Spine-B for the equivalence between HR and betting families.

Pythia/PowerAnalysis.lean.regime: Type-II power loss; b, s, sigma, delta parameters
Pythia/PowerAnalysis.lean.rationale: Power-loss (Type-II error) analogue of quantization-slack theorem. Spine-B: infrastructure for the deployment-slack framework. Corrected version with h_slack hypothesis.

Pythia/Quantization.lean.regime: quantization of reals to scale s; family-specific eta rates
Pythia/Quantization.lean.rationale: Spine infrastructure: quantization-transport lemma + all four family eta rates + four-way ranking. In AxiomAudit. Backbone of the deployment-slack framework.

Pythia/Quantum/VonNeumannEntropyNonnegTw.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: S(rho) >= 0 for qubit.

Pythia/Queueing/ErlangB.lean.regime: M/M/n/n Erlang loss queue; n servers, offered load rho >= 0
Pythia/Queueing/ErlangB.lean.rationale: Erlang B formula for teletraffic engineering. Core queueing result used in capacity planning. Probability bounds and recursion are substantive. BORDERLINE: some results proved, recursion may be scaffold.

Pythia/Queueing/LittlesLaw.lean.regime: sample-path queueing system; limits arr_rate, W, L exist
Pythia/Queueing/LittlesLaw.lean.rationale: Sample-path Little's Law: L = lambda*W via product-of-limits rule. Full proof via Filter.Tendsto.mul and tendsto_nhds_unique. BORDERLINE vs OR/LittlesLaw.lean (different level).

Pythia/Risk/CVaR.lean.regime: Rockafellar-Uryasev CVaR; alpha in (0,1); integrable loss X
Pythia/Risk/CVaR.lean.rationale: CVaR dual representation (Rockafellar-Uryasev). Basel III/IV regulatory risk metric. User-facing financial risk management result. Several results may be scaffold-sorrys.

Pythia/Risk/CoherentMeasures.lean.regime: ADEH coherent risk measures; finite Omega; rho : (Omega -> R) -> R
Pythia/Risk/CoherentMeasures.lean.rationale: ADEH coherent measures representation theorem on finite Omega. In AxiomAudit. Financial risk management. Finite Omega makes this tractable.

Pythia/SPRT.lean.regime: SPRT with log-LR threshold A > 0 > B; iid p0 or p1
Pythia/SPRT.lean.rationale: Wald SPRT error-rate theorems. error_rates is closed; wald_approximation, wald_wolfowitz_optimal, expected_sample_size are honest-sorrys with detailed closure plans. Core sequential statistics result.

Pythia/Scratch/SDKFleetDemo/BSCallUpperB.rationale: SDK fleet demo scratch file. Contains invalid Lean syntax (backtick code block in proof body - not valid Lean 4). Scratch/demo infrastructure, not library content.
SDK fleet demo scratch file. bs_call_upper_bound is a valid closed proof but lives in Scratch/SDKFleetDemo namespace. Not part of the canonical library surface. Demo infrastructure.

Pythia/Scratch/SwarmDogfood/BonferroniFw.rationale: Swarm dogfood scratch file. bonferroni_fwer_test is a restatement+proof of the theorem from HypothesisTest/MultipleTesting.lean (the one with sorry there, actually closed here). Scratch/demo infrastructure.

Pythia/Sharpness.lean.regime: sharpness witnesses for four CS families; b >= 2, s >= 1, sigma > 0
Pythia/Sharpness.lean.rationale: Existence-of-deviation sharpness witnesses. Each theorem proves max(...,0) >= 0 (trivially). The measure-theoretic adversary construction is informal. Spine-B infrastructure.

Pythia/Stochastic/ItoIsometryFiniteDim.l.regime: f : Fin n -> R; discrete finite-dimensional analogue
Pythia/Stochastic/ItoIsometryFiniteDim.l.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: (sum f_i)^2 <= n * sum (f_i)^2.

Pythia/StochasticApproximation/Dvoretzky.regime: stochastic approximation with step sizes a_n; summable b_n perturbations
Pythia/StochasticApproximation/Dvoretzky.rationale: Dvoretzky convergence theorem + Robbins-Monro + SGD convergence. Core stochastic approximation results used in ML/RL. In AxiomAudit. Shares stochastic_approximation spine with RobbinsMonro and RobbinsSiegmund.

Pythia/StochasticApproximation/RobbinsMo.regime: Robbins-Monro SA; step sizes sum=inf, sum-sq<inf; conditional mean = f(X_n)
Pythia/StochasticApproximation/RobbinsMo.rationale: Robbins-Monro (1951) stochastic approximation convergence. In AxiomAudit. Foundation of iterative statistical estimation and SGD. Shares stochastic_approximation spine.

Pythia/StochasticApproximation/RobbinsSi.regime: non-negative adapted process; summable alpha_n, beta_n; E[V_{n+1}|F_n] <= (1+alpha_n)V_n + beta_n
Pythia/StochasticApproximation/RobbinsSi.rationale: Robbins-Siegmund almost-supermartingale convergence. In AxiomAudit. Core convergence lemma for stochastic approximation algorithms. Shares stochastic_approximation spine.

Pythia/StoppingRule.lean.rationale: StoppingRule structure + Admissible structure + firstHit definition. Infrastructure; no theorems.

Pythia/SubGamma.lean.regime: sub-gamma martingale with parameters (nu, c); c*|lam| < 1
Pythia/SubGamma.lean.rationale: Sub-gamma tail class generalizing sub-Gaussian. Has structural sorrys (excluded from public surface per API.lean). Spine-B: shares concentration_iid_bounded with Bernstein, Bennett.

Pythia/SubGaussianMG.lean.regime: sub-Gaussian martingale; sigma > 0; finite-measure probability space
Pythia/SubGaussianMG.lean.rationale: Core sub-Gaussian MG structure + Ville's inequality. Spine infrastructure for BettingCS, HowardRamdasCS, SPRT. In AxiomAudit. Backbone of the library.

Pythia/Tactic/AnytimeValid.lean.rationale: anytime_valid tactic implementation. Pure tactic plumbing; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/AnytimeValidRegistry.lean.rationale: anytime_valid_lemma registry. @[anytime_valid_lemma] attribute registrations. Pure registry/infrastructure.

Pythia/Tactic/CSFamilyAttr.lean.rationale: Attribute-driven registry for CSFamily declarations. Pure Lean infrastructure; no empirical content.

Pythia/Tactic/CSFamilyRegistry.lean.rationale: attribute [cs_family] registration for four canonical CS families. Registry glue.

Pythia/Tactic/CVC5Check.lean.rationale: cvc5_check tactic implementation. SMT oracle bridge; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/Disprove.lean.rationale: disprove tactic implementation. Counterexample-finder; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/DomainCalculator.lean.rationale: Generic domain-calculator typeclass. Pure def/structure/class; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/ECheck.lean.rationale: e_check tactic implementation. E prover oracle bridge; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/FlagConcreteConstants.lean.rationale: #flag_concrete_constants command. LLM-defense compile-time command; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/MinimizeHypotheses.lean.rationale: #minimize_hypotheses command. LLM-defense compile-time command; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/ProbSimp.lean.rationale: prob_simp/pdf_simp tactic + @[prob_simp] attribute. Tactic plumbing; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/ProbSimpRegistry.lean.rationale: @[prob_simp] membership table. Registry of existing Mathlib lemmas; no new theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/Pythia.lean.rationale: pythia tactic + @[stat_lemma] attribute declaration. Core tactic plumbing; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/PythiaBang.lean.rationale: pythia! / pythia? hammer ladder orchestrator. Tactic plumbing; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/StatSimp.lean.rationale: stat_simp tactic + @[stat_simp] attribute. Tactic plumbing; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/StatSimpRegistry.lean.rationale: @[stat_simp] membership table. Registry of existing Mathlib lemmas; no new theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/StatsIneq.lean.rationale: stats_ineq tactic + @[stats_ineq] attribute. Tactic plumbing; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/StatsIneqRegistry.lean.rationale: @[stats_ineq] membership table. Registry of existing lemmas; no new theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/TPTPEncode.lean.rationale: TPTP encoder for FOL oracle adapters. Shared encoder infrastructure; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/TightTail.lean.rationale: Tail-bound calculator tactic. Evaluates registered inequalities numerically; pure def/eval infrastructure.

Pythia/Tactic/VampireCheck.lean.rationale: vampire_check tactic. Vampire FOL oracle bridge; no theorems.

Pythia/Tactic/VilleCmd.lean.rationale: #ville elaborator command. Prints CSFamily fields; pure infrastructure command.

Pythia/Tactic/Z3Check.lean.rationale: z3_check tactic. Z3 SMT oracle bridge; no theorems.

Pythia/Thermodynamics/CarnotEfficiencyUp.rationale: Already in theorem_manifest. Closed result: eta = 1 - T_c/T_h <= 1.

Pythia/TimeSeries/NeweyWest.lean.regime: iid with E[X]=0, E[X^2]=sigma^2; bandwidth m(T) -> inf, m(T)/T^{1/4} -> 0
Pythia/TimeSeries/NeweyWest.lean.rationale: Newey-West HAC variance estimator consistency. In AxiomAudit. Fundamental for heteroskedasticity-robust inference in econometrics/time series. Shares time_series spine with WoldDecomposition.

Pythia/TimeSeries/WoldDecomposition.lean.regime: zero-mean covariance-stationary process in L^2; shift isometry U
Pythia/TimeSeries/WoldDecomposition.lean.rationale: Wold decomposition: MA(inf) + deterministic component. Core time-series result. Several results proved (unitarySubspace_isClosed, wold_decomposition). Shares time_series spine.

Pythia/TimeUniformCLT.lean.regime: iid with finite second moment sigma^2; Levy-Prokhorov metric; all t
Pythia/TimeUniformCLT.lean.rationale: Time-uniform CLT (WSSR24). All scaffold-sorrys pending Levy-Prokhorov machinery. User-facing: working statisticians test time-uniform CLT empirically. BORDERLINE: entirely sorry'd.

Pythia/VectorSharpness.lean.regime: vector-valued sub-Gaussian MG; 1-d projection; Gaussian boundary density
Pythia/VectorSharpness.lean.rationale: Sharpness infrastructure for vector CS family. All sorry'd. Spine-B: needed to complete 4-of-4 sharp-constant pinning for Theorem 1. Part of confidence_sequence spine.

Pythia/VilleMathlibPR.lean.rationale: House-style reference draft for Mathlib PR. Uses Lean 4 module syntax incompatible with Lake (public import). Explicitly excluded from public surface per API.lean. Not library content.

Pythia/VilleSupermartingale.lean.rationale: Ville's inequality corollaries for non-negative supermartingales. Spine-B: infrastructure for SubGaussianMG, BettingCS, SPRT.

Pythia/WaldIdentity.lean.regime: iid integrable X_i; stopping time tau with E[tau] < inf
Pythia/WaldIdentity.lean.rationale: Wald's identity: E[sum_{i<=tau} X_i] = E[X_1]*E[tau]. Scaffold-sorrys with closure plans. Core sequential statistics result used in SPRT. Shares confidence_sequence spine.
